select_when: Progress is not linear and one constrained variable or threshold could unlock disproportionate change.
The system has resisted many incremental fixes and the real question is where leverage actually lives.
The team needs to identify the condition that turns a marginal effort into a self-reinforcing shift.
A seemingly impossible idea might work if the hidden enabling threshold can be named and tested.
avoid_when: Leaders are romanticizing breakthrough moments while underinvesting in the slow prerequisite buildup required for a real threshold crossing.
The team is treating a symptom as the tipping point instead of diagnosing the deeper causal constraint.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the file on thresholds, leverage, and what-must-be-true testing rather than on generic breakthrough rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader persuasion and performance-spike sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on threshold conditions and leverage.
open_questions: The source overlaps with communication and organizational change, but the operational file stays on locating the threshold that changes the system.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on nonlinearity, leverage, threshold conditions, and what-would-need-to-be-true framing.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against romanticizing breakthroughs and against treating symptoms instead of deep constraints.

input_type.note: The typed input label compresses the threshold-sensitive activation conditions.

output_type.note: The output label captures tipping-points as a leverage and threshold hypothesis rather than a guarantee of breakthrough.

reasoning_types.note: The model relies on causal leverage tracing inside dynamic systems.
